text.title: Spring @Async Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real Spring @Async deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: OOM under load; thread dump shows thousands of `SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor` threads with no pool reuse.
antipattern.root-cause: `@Async` without an executor name falls back to `SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor`, which spawns a new thread per call.
antipattern.fix: Define a named `ThreadPoolTaskExecutor` per workload SLA; annotate every `@Async` method with the executor name.

antipattern.symptom: Async email never sends; no error in logs; users complain weeks later.
antipattern.root-cause: `@Async` method returns `void`; the thrown exception is swallowed because no `AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler` is registered.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `CompletableFuture<Void>` and let the caller observe failure; or register `AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler` if `void` is unavoidable.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint blocks for the duration of the "async" work; profile shows the call running synchronously on the request thread.
antipattern.root-cause: Method `foo()` calls `this.barAsync()`; the call bypasses the AOP proxy that would have switched threads.
antipattern.fix: Inject a separate bean and call its async method, or use `AopContext.currentProxy()` with `@EnableAspectJAutoProxy(exposeProxy = true)`.

antipattern.symptom: Logs in async tasks have empty `requestId`; correlation across the request breaks.
antipattern.root-cause: Executor not wrapped in a `TaskDecorator` that copies MDC + `SecurityContext` from the calling thread.
antipattern.fix: Implement `TaskDecorator` capturing MDC + `SecurityContext` before dispatch; set it on every executor.

antipattern.symptom: Async work runs after the parent transaction commits OR rolls back independently; partial-failure data corruption.
antipattern.root-cause: `@Async` opens a new thread; `@Transactional` does not propagate across thread boundaries.
antipattern.fix: Move the `@Async` call outside the transaction, or use `TransactionSynchronizationManager.registerSynchronization(...)` to fire after commit.
